p.seg: Robert of London sends greeting to all sons of the Church, both clergy as well as laity.

Be it known to you that by this my present charter I have confirmed my father's charter by which he gave and confirmed to God and to St Mary at Melrose half a ploughgate of land. I have added and applied to that half ploughgate of land the rest of the land which lies contiguous as far as 'Derestredt' and as the road descends in a slanting direction on the east down to the stream. All these things I have granted and given for the salvation of my soul, and for the soul of Thomas my father's brother, and for the souls of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors.

With these witnesses: William son of Odard, and Henry son of Jordan; William the clerk, of Chausi; Roger the clerk, who wrote this charter; Richard de Cameis, and Machus the provost; and Osmund my servant.
